format_discipline.notes: No YAML frontmatter; first non-empty line is '# Using the TensorFlow Embedding Projector to Explore Word Clusters'. reading.meta.json exists, parses cleanly, and carries all required fields (word_count 1240, estimated_read_minutes 7, citations_used [1,3], sections). Exactly 6 sections in correct order: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References. The embedded mermaid block (lines 21-50) is expected (embeds_diagram=true) and is excluded from section/word accounting.

length_budget.notes: 1240 words (mermaid code block excluded) is within the [1200, 1600] standard-profile target. Read time at 200 wpm (artifact-mix canonical) = 6.2 min, inside the 6-8 min window; at 230 wpm = 5.4 min, marginally under the prompt's 6-8 reference but the binding gate is word count, which is in range. meta.json word_count consistent with body estimate.

citation_discipline.notes: [1] (primary, research.google) cited repeatedly: Overview (line 5), Key Concepts (9, 16, 53, 55, 57), In Practice (73, 79), Key Takeaways implied, References (108). [3] (applied, tensorflow.org) cited: Key Concepts (9, 11, 57), In Practice lab (90), References (109). Both citations_used markers [1] and [3] resolve to bibliography entries; no orphan markers, no orphan bibliography entries. Supporting resource [2] is not primary and is not required to be cited.

sequence_drift.notes: Concepts used are this topic's own (Projector, squashing, dimension, cluster, search, nearest neighbours) or prior-topic vocabulary (vector/list-of-numbers 6.3, point-in-space 6.5, cosine similarity 6.9). PCA and t-SNE are name-and-defer only: presented as 'named buttons that do the squashing' with 'how they actually work is covered later in the curriculum' (line 19) and Key Takeaways (104) — no defining clause teaching their internals. Embedding-training also deferred ('no studying how embeddings are trained', line 104). Bold on 'PCA'/'t-SNE' is as UI button labels, not as a definition of the methods, consistent with corpus §5.2. No forward references found.

beginner_readability.notes: Terms defined on first use in bold-term/plain-meaning shape: 'TensorFlow Embedding Projector' (line 9), 'Dimension - one number, one direction' (15), 'Cluster - a clump of dots' (55), 'Search' (57). Concrete-before-abstract throughout (sculpture-photo analogy precedes the squashing abstraction; apple/iphone example grounds the search concept). Sequences rendered as numbered lists (Worked Example 61-67, lab 92-96), comparison as a do/don't table (83-88), inline examples present outside the Worked Example section. Paragraphs <=4 sentences. No hedging. No assessment references leaked into learner prose (corpus §9 'Assessment 2 is due this week' correctly omitted from the reading). 0 readability + 0 formatting findings; below the 3-finding escalation threshold.

faithfulness.notes: 10 assertions sampled and verified against topic_corpus.md: (1) Projector is free browser tool, dots = words, position from embedding vector - corpus §5.1; (2) TensorFlow is a Google toolkit, no need to know it - §5.1; (3) loads with example dataset, explore day one - §5.1; (4) 200-number embedding => 200-dimension space, screen has at most 3 - §5.2; (5) squashing keeps close points close, sculpture-photo analogy - §5.2; (6) PCA/t-SNE rearrange dots but clusters survive - §5.2; (7) cluster = clump, closeness = similarity, eye spots grouping - §5.3; (8) search lights nearest neighbours, side panel with cosine-similarity number - §5.4; (9) apple => apples/fruit/banana/iphone, embeddings learn from usage - §5.4; (10) diagnostic tool for ML teams, search/recommendations/grouping applications - §7. Mermaid diagram flow (long vectors -> squash -> dot cloud -> clusters, with search->nearest-neighbour callout) matches corpus §5 flow and artifact_plan diagram_intent. 0 unsupported assertions; curated walkthrough of corpus content, no new claims.

verdict: PASS - all 6 checks passed; 1240 words in range, [1] and [3] cited, PCA/t-SNE and embedding-training name-and-defer only, mermaid diagram faithful to corpus §5 flow, zero readability/formatting/scope findings.
